name: Iberian Route
name_original: Ruta Íbera — circular desde Ariño - Norte Teruel (BTT09)
description: This 22.3-mile mountain biking route from Ariño offers moderate difficulty, panoramic views, and ancient rock paintings.
region_intro: Mountain bike trails around Ariño traverse a diverse landscape characterized by the Martín River valley and the foothills of the Sierra de Arcos. The region features varied topography, including riverine areas and more challenging mountainous terrain. Natural features like the Cueva Foradada Reservoir contribute to the scenic routes available for mountain bikers. Best mountain bike trails around Ariño The most popular mountain bike trail is Iberian Route, a 22.3 miles (35.8 km) trail that takes 3 hours 19 minutes to complete. This moderate route offers a journey through the historical Iberian landscape. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is Sierra de Arcos, Ariño and mining park — circular from Andorra (BTT02), a moderate 30.2 miles (48.6 km) path. This circular route explores the Sierra de Arcos and includes historical mining park areas. Local mountain bikers also love the Martín River Route (BTT 01), a 22.3 miles (35.9 km) trail leading through the Martín River valley, often completed in about 3 hours 3 minutes. Mountain biking around Ariño is defined by river valleys, mountainous terrain, and reservoir views. The network offers options for different ability levels, with all listed routes being of moderate difficulty. The routes in Ariño are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.0 stars from more than 5 reviews. More than 60 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Ariño's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many mountain bike trails are there in Ariño?
A: Ariño offers a selection of 13 mountain bike trails, all of which are rated as moderate difficulty. These routes traverse diverse landscapes, including the Martín River valley and the foothills of the Sierra de Arcos.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on mountain bike trails around Ariño?
A: The mountain bike trails around Ariño feature a varied topography. You'll encounter riverine areas along the Martín River valley, as well as more challenging mountainous terrain in the foothills of the Sierra de Arcos. Natural features like the Cueva Foradada Reservoir also contribute to the scenic routes.

Q: Are there any circular mountain bike routes in Ariño?
A: Yes, Ariño offers several circular mountain bike routes. A notable option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e996652736/sierra-de-arcos-arino-and-mining-park-circular-from-andorra-btt02" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sierra de Arcos, Ariño and mining park — circular from Andorra (BTT02)&lt;/a&gt;, which is 48.6 km long and explores both the Sierra de Arcos and historical mining park areas. Another circular option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20143527" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Forest Trail loop from Albalate del Arzobispo&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What are some interesting landmarks or natural features to see along the mountain bike trails?
A: While mountain biking around Ariño, you can encounter several interesting sights. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3618839" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cueva Foradada Reservoir&lt;/a&gt; offers picturesque views. You might also pass by the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5285525" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sanctuary of the Virgin of Arcos&lt;/a&gt;, a historical-artistic monument, or enjoy a &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5361235" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of Alloza and the Church of the Purísima Concepción&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What is the difficulty level of mountain bike trails in Ariño?
A: All mountain bike trails listed for Ariño are of moderate difficulty. This means they are suitable for riders with some experience, offering a good balance of challenge and accessibility across varied terrain.

Q: What do other mountain bikers say about the trails in Ariño?
A: The mountain bike trails in Ariño are highly regarded by the komoot community, with an average rating of 4.0 stars from over 5 reviews. More than 60 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore the region's varied terrain, suggesting a positive and engaging experience.

Q: Are there any routes that follow the Martín River?
A: Yes, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e996649204/martin-river-route-btt-01" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Martín River Route (BTT 01)&lt;/a&gt; is a 35.9 km trail that leads directly through the scenic Martín River valley, offering a moderate ride of about 3 hours and 3 minutes.

Q: What is a good route for exploring the historical landscape near Ariño?
A: For a journey through the historical Iberian landscape, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e999036855/iberian-route" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Iberian Route&lt;/a&gt; is an excellent choice. This moderate 35.8 km trail typically takes around 3 hours and 19 minutes to complete.

Q: Are there any mountain bike routes that connect to other towns or areas?
A: Yes, routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e996655190/andorra-to-escuriza-reservoir-trail-btt-04" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Andorra to Escuriza Reservoir Trail BTT 04&lt;/a&gt; connect Ariño's surroundings with nearby areas, offering longer rides and diverse scenery. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e996652736/sierra-de-arcos-arino-and-mining-park-circular-from-andorra-btt02" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sierra de Arcos, Ariño and mining park — circular from Andorra (BTT02)&lt;/a&gt; also starts from Andorra.

Q: What is the best time of year for mountain biking in Ariño?
A: While specific seasonal recommendations are not provided, the diverse landscapes of river valleys and mountainous terrain suggest that spring and autumn would likely offer the most pleasant conditions for mountain biking, avoiding the extreme heat of summer or potential winter chill.

Q: Are there any routes that feature reservoirs?
A: Yes, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e996655190/andorra-to-escuriza-reservoir-trail-btt-04" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Andorra to Escuriza Reservoir Trail BTT 04&lt;/a&gt; specifically includes the Escuriza Reservoir. Additionally, the Cueva Foradada Reservoir is a prominent natural feature in the region, likely offering scenic views along various routes.

Q: What is the longest mountain bike trail available in Ariño?
A: The longest mountain bike trail listed is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e996652736/sierra-de-arcos-arino-and-mining-park-circular-from-andorra-btt02" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sierra de Arcos, Ariño and mining park — circular from Andorra (BTT02)&lt;/a&gt;, which spans 48.6 km and involves an elevation gain of approximately 744 meters.
tips: Nice stretch of path parallel to the Martín River

The curious nature gives us this beautiful "well" 120 meters deep located to the south of the Sierra de Arcos that is undoubtedly worth a visit. Near the Sima de San Pedro we find the beautiful Iberian settlement of Cabezo de San Pedro.

Located between the towns of Oliete and Ariño, we find one of the most particular archaeological sites in Aragon due to its location on a cliff, the wall and its towers. And also very close we can visit the Sima de San Pedro.

We are in the beautiful mining town of Ariño located at the foot of the Sierra de Arcos and with many points of interest such as the Mining Museum, a spring, the parish church of San Salvador or the hermitage of Santa Bárbara among many others, so if we have time, it is definitely worth a visit.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the Ruta Íbera (BTT09) mountain biking route?
faqs.answer: The route begins in Ariño. While specific parking areas aren't detailed, information points are typically available at the start of each route within the Espacio BTT NORTETERUEL network, which would likely include parking facilities.

faqs.question: Is the Ruta Íbera (BTT09) suitable for beginner mountain bikers?
faqs.answer: This route is classified as moderate and requires good physical condition and experience with mountain biking. Its technical difficulty is approximated at STS S2, meaning it's generally not recommended for absolute beginners.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on this mountain biking route?
faqs.answer: The route features varied terrain, starting with a constant ascent towards 'El Puerto' and then winding around the majestic Cerro Felio. You'll encounter open environments shared with forestry, livestock, and agricultural activities, situated between the foothills of the Iberian System and the Ebro Depression.

faqs.question: What can I see along the Ruta Íbera (BTT09)?
faqs.answer: Along the route, you'll experience panoramic views of Ariño and Alacón. Notable features include the Río Martín Trail, the Sima de San Pedro, and the Iberian Settlement of Cabezo de San Pedro. The area around Cerro Felio is also known for numerous Levantine rock paintings. You'll also pass by the San Salvador Parish Church Bell Tower.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to mountain bike the Ruta Íbera (BTT09)?
faqs.answer: Given its location in Teruel, Spain, spring and autumn generally offer the most pleasant weather for mountain biking, with milder temperatures. Summers can be very hot, and winters may bring colder conditions, though the trail is accessible year-round.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or entrance fees required to ride this trail?
faqs.answer: No, the Ruta Íbera (BTT09) is part of the Espacio BTT NORTETERUEL network, which comprises well-marked, free-access circular BTT routes. You do not need permits or to pay entrance fees.

faqs.question: Is the Ruta Íbera (BTT09) mountain biking route dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While specific regulations for dogs on this particular route are not detailed, generally, dogs are allowed on trails in the region. It's always best practice to keep your dog on a leash, especially given the presence of livestock and wildlife, and to clean up after them.

faqs.question: Does this route intersect with other trails?
faqs.answer: Yes, the Ruta Íbera (BTT09) covers a significant portion of the official 'Ruta Íbera' trail (90%) and also passes through sections of 'Por el río Martín' (51%) and 'Sendero del Río Martín' (33%).

faqs.question: Are there any specific regulations or fees for mountain biking within the Parque Cultural del Río Martín?
faqs.answer: The Ruta Íbera (BTT09) is a free-access route within the Espacio BTT NORTETERUEL network. While it is located in the Parque Cultural del Río Martín, there are no specific fees for mountain biking this trail. However, always respect general park regulations, such as staying on marked paths and protecting the natural and cultural heritage.

faqs.question: How well-marked is the Ruta Íbera (BTT09)?
faqs.answer: The route is clearly signposted throughout. You'll find vertical signs at main intersections and directional markers to guide riders, ensuring you stay on track. Detailed track information can also be downloaded.

faqs.question: What kind of views can I expect along the trail?
faqs.answer: The trail offers diverse and scenic views. From the constant ascent towards 'El Puerto,' you'll get panoramic vistas of Ariño and Alacón. The route also provides open landscapes shared with forestry and agricultural activities, set against the backdrop of the Iberian System foothills and the Ebro Depression.
